name: Boswellia
summary: Boswellia entry from SciSpace evidence pass. Evidence level: moderate.
description: Boswellia (boswellic acids) is mechanistically tied to lipoxygenase modulation, aligning with leukotriene-pathway suppression models for inflammatory processes.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.7: Boswellia/AKBA has human OA evidence, but product standardization is critical.
claimMap.notes: v9.7: Boswellia/AKBA has human OA evidence, but product standardization is critical.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Boswellia extract dosing varies; AKBA-standardized extracts often use 100–250 mg/day in OA studies

claimMap.claim: Combination trial; do not isolate Boswellia effect
claimMap.notes: Combination trial; do not isolate Boswellia effect

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Curcuma longa plus Boswellia serrata extract combination; 3 months

contraindications: Known allergy to boswellia or other Burseraceae-family plants
concurrent warfarin or aspirin or other anticoagulant or antiplatelet medication due to a theoretical additive bleeding risk
concurrent immunosuppressant medication such as cyclosporine due to boswellia's immune-modulating activity
pregnancy due to traditional use as a uterine stimulant and insufficient safety data
severe liver or kidney disease due to insufficient safety data in these populations.
